rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Production Of Responsive Documents; Requests For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 p.m., Line 1. Defendant Campbell Soup Company¿s motion to compel production of documents and request for sanctions is granted in part. If they have not already done so, plaintiffs must produce all responsive documents no later than February 15. Defendant¿s request to strike the objections made on the grounds of vague, ambiguous, overbreadth, disjunctive, compound and collateral source rule is granted. All objections stand. Defendant is awarded sanctions in the amount of $1840 (six ours t $300 per hour plus a $40 filing fee) due to the lack of substantial justification by plaintiffs in failing to produce the requested documents. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GILBERT FORBES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Seeking In Camera Review Of Records Produced By 3m Company, By Defendant, Asbestos Corporation Ltd. [Evidence Code, Section 915; Crc 2.551]
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 p.m., Line 2. Defendant Asbestos Corp. Ltd.¿s motion seeking in camera review of records produced by 3M Co. is granted as plaintiff does not oppose defendant¿s motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 p.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel Schneider Electric¿s further response to request, demand inspection, production of documents was continued from January 27 to give the parties an opportunity to engage in further meet and confer efforts to narrow the scope of the information sought from defendants to a reasonably defined product or products attributable to defendant that plaintiffs in good faith contend that Mr. Gauger was exposed to. If all issues raised by this motion are not resolved before then, by 4:30pm today the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, what issues remain and then must appear at the hearing at 9am on February 8. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Crane Co.S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Specially Prepared Interrogatory No. 3.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 p.m., Line 4. Defendant Crane Co.'s motion To Compel Further Responses To Specially Prepared Interrogatory No. 3 is off calendar at the request of the moving party. = (220/HEK)
